content: Phase III success criteria require: statistically significant improvement on pre-specified primary endpoint(s) with appropriate multiplicity adjustment, consistent results across key subgroups, safety profile acceptable relative to benefit, and demonstration of clinical relevance (not just statistical significance). Regulatory agencies require at least two adequate and well-controlled Phase III trials showing effectiveness, or one exceptionally robust trial with confirmatory evidence. Non-inferiority trials must show the lower bound of the confidence interval exceeds the pre-specified margin. Common failure reasons include insufficient effect size, safety concerns emerging in larger populations, or failure to replicate Phase II results.
